classification.typology_description: Likely synthetic / borrowed identity — high-risk flag set, low-income occupation profile, and a very recently opened account.

behavioural_indicators.label: Dormancy Signal
behavioural_indicators.description: Low account tenure / dormancy-break signal. Mule median 41 vs legit 64.

behavioural_indicators.label: Activity Flag
behavioural_indicators.description: Elevated transactional activity. Mules score ~22% higher (0.72 vs 0.59).

behavioural_indicators.label: Legitimacy Gap
behavioural_indicators.description: Missing legitimacy indicator. Every confirmed mule has F2082 = 0.

behavioural_indicators.label: Missingness Pattern
behavioural_indicators.description: Activity-count field absent. 82.7% of mules missing it vs 64% of legit.

behavioural_indicators.label: High-Risk Flag
behavioural_indicators.description: F670 = 1 carries a 2.29% mule rate (2.6x the population average).

behavioural_indicators.label: Risk Score
behavioural_indicators.description: Normalised transaction risk score derived from F115.

ai_report:
**EXECUTIVE SUMMARY**

This report investigates the Alpha-042 account, which has been flagged for high risk due to a score of 94.0/100 and a critical severity level. The model risk probability is 0.9980, indicating a strong likelihood of fraud. A match was found in the Fraud Registry (F3912), further confirming the elevated risk.

**RISK ASSESSMENT**

The account's risk profile is characterized by a high-risk flag (F670) with a SHAP value of +0.290, increasing the risk by 29%. The Legitimacy Indicator (F2082) also shows a positive SHAP value (+0.180), contributing to the overall risk assessment. These indicators suggest that the account may be subject to synthetic identity fraud.

**KEY FRAUD INDICATORS**

The following key fraud indicators have been identified:
• Fraud Registry Flag (Leakage Warning) (F3912): +0.460
• High-Risk Flag (F670): +0.290
• Legitimacy Indicator (F2082): +0.180
• Account Tenure (F2956): +0.150
• Account Holder Occupation (F3891): +0.110

These indicators collectively increase the risk assessment, indicating a high likelihood of fraud.

**MULE TYPOLOGY ASSESSMENT**

The account has been classified as Synthetic Identity with a confidence level of 97%. The matched typology evidence includes:
• Fraud registry match (F3912=1)
• High-risk flag set (F670=1)
• Low-income occupation (student/agriculture)
• Very new account (low tenure)

These characteristics are consistent with synthetic identity fraud, where an individual creates a false identity to obtain credit or other financial services.

**ACCOUNT PROFILE ANALYSIS**

The account holder's profile is as follows:
• Occupation: agriculture
• Account standing period: L180D
• Account holder age: 31.0
• Account tenure: 22.0

The low-income occupation and very new account tenure contribute to the overall risk assessment, suggesting that the individual may be vulnerable to exploitation.

**RECOMMENDED ACTIONS**

Based on this investigation, we recommend:
• Immediate flagging of the account for further review
• Notification of senior management and compliance team
• Initiation of enhanced due diligence procedures
• Review of transaction activity for suspicious behavior

These actions will help mitigate potential losses and ensure regulatory compliance.

**REGULATORY NOTE**

This report is in accordance with Bank of India's Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Combating the Financing of Terrorism (CFT) policies. The findings and recommendations are based on the structured analysis provided, and all necessary steps will be taken to address the identified risks.
